title: Structured signal conversion pipeline: SIG-1..SIG-41 full audit
method: Classified all 41 structured signals (SIG-1 through SIG-41) by type, source, priority, status, time-to-action, target specificity, and whether conversion was same-session or cross-session. Distinguished true pipeline conversion (signal drove future action) from self-resolved bookkeeping (signal documented current work).

H1_conversion_rate.verdict: NUANCED — headline FALSIFIED, true pipeline rate CONFIRMED

H2_time_to_action.verdict: FALSIFIED — converted signals act in 0-1 sessions, not 15+

H3_target_specificity.actual: 0/13 unconverted directives name a specific target (100% separation)
H3_target_specificity.verdict: CONFIRMED — target specificity is a perfect predictor

same_session_self_resolve.note: Signal documents current work, not pipeline conversion

cross_session_1_2.note: True pipeline: signal drives action in next 1-2 sessions

long_term_partial.note: SIG-1/2/7: created S340-S341, still only PARTIALLY resolved after 57+ sessions

ai_directives_only.note: Excluding observations, closest to true pipeline rate

P1.note: But SIG-1/2 took 57+ sessions, SIG-39 still OPEN at 2 sessions

converted_signals.note: Bimodal: 0-1 sessions or never. No 3-56 session range.

target_specificity.note: 0/13 vs 8/12 — target specificity is near-perfect predictor. Confirms L-635 (15% conversion for abstract targets).

resolvable_open_signals.note: L-811 pattern repeats: resolution-tracking failure is systematic, not one-time

key_findings: 58.5% headline conversion masks bimodal reality: 39% same-session self-resolve + 17% true cross-session pipeline + 41% never
Time-to-action is bimodal (0-1 sessions or never) with zero signals in 3-56 session range — the gap is empty
Target specificity is a near-perfect predictor: 0/13 unconverted directives name a specific tool/file vs 8/12 converted
Human signals convert 80% vs AI directives 37% — human specificity advantage
13/17 OPEN signals are resolvable from existing work — L-811 resolution-tracking failure is chronic, not episodic
P1 priority does NOT accelerate conversion: SIG-1/2 partial after 57s, SIG-39 OPEN at 2s

comparison_to_prior.S313_baseline: 39% canonical encoding (111 broad signals). This study: 17% true cross-session pipeline (41 structured signals). Different methodology, compatible findings.
comparison_to_prior.S365_remeasure: 42.1% (+3.1pp). Format=mechanism (section 89.7% vs table 39.0%). This study confirms: same-session format drives conversion, not priority.
comparison_to_prior.S397_L811: Signal audit resolved 11 signals. This audit resolves 13 more. Pattern: chronic resolution-tracking failure.

prescriptions: Require target specificity at signal creation: reject signals without tool/file/frontier target (L-635 at signal level)
Auto-resolve observation-type signals at creation (born-resolved pattern, 80% of observations)
Add resolution-tracking to swarm_signal.py: flag signals >10 sessions old for mandatory review
P1 priority is cosmetic: enforce P1→immediate-action routing, not just labeling
